title: UserPromptSubmit hook for memory injection
userStory: As a user, I want my Claude Code prompts pre-augmented with relevant brain hits so that I do not have to mention the brain or its sources explicitly.
acceptanceCriteria: inject_memory.py reads the prompt from stdin (Claude Code hook input JSON)
Optional scoping: if BRAIN_INJECT_SCOPE is set, the hook no-ops when cwd is not under that path; if unset, the hook always runs
Calls Graphiti search_nodes(query=prompt, limit=5) with an 800ms timeout
On hits, emits hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext with a <relevant-memory> block listing the top 5 entries
On timeout or error, logs to .claude/logs/inject_memory.log under cwd and exits 0 (silent degrade)
Client repo's .claude/settings.json adds a UserPromptSubmit hook entry pointing at the script
blockedReason: Same scope denial as US-012. Hook script lives at templates/claude-code-client/inject_memory.py; install steps in templates/claude-code-client/INSTALL.md (step 3).
